description: Ansys Cloud is great for large simulation cases. For me a CFD case with millions of mesh elements would mostly benefit from this software.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The most important feature about this software is the vision that ANSYS has towards the future of simulation. A user/company could setup its cases locally then submit them to the cloud for fast/accurate results. This convenience removes the large initial cost on the user who will pay only for the resources used.
cons: For now I don't any issue or bugs with Ansys Cloud. I only think the cost is a bit high compared to Cloud vendor like AWS or Azure. It should be noted however, that Ansys Cloud offers some degree of automation when exporting the works to the cloud.

description: Really happy to use this software without it a lot of designs and analytics could be more hard to get.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ansys is a most powerful and easy program even if it's not that easy to learn but still giving a lot of excellent options and especially details.
cons: Little hard to learn all what the software is capable to do may take some time, and also need some secs or minutes to work sometimes.

review_title: Good entry, but could use some work
description: It seems like a nice entry into the cloud solution space, but could use some work on polishing the implementation.
author: Stuart B.
pros: Easy to set up and submit a job, nice reporting interface online
cons: Not easy to recover data and integrate it with local solutions. Rather than having to download a standalone file, it would be nice if the data could be streamed back into the original solution, just as if you had run the simulation locally. Having to manage multiple version of the same file is a large pain.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I am happy with ANSYS cloud.
author: Sudipta M.
pros: Flexibility of using multiple cores and memory.
cons: File transfer can be nightmare sometime.

description: It was really fast and valuable to experience such an opportunity.
author: Alaettin O.
pros: Easy to use, fast with multiple cpu s and rams, optional things to select if want.
cons: Still has bugs in it. When i use it, I could not see real time elastic coins. Responses from operation system may be slow.

author: Antonio L.
pros: What we DEMAND, please, a more effective training docs comprehensible to whatever new graduate!
cons: The Object Oriented Structure as Simulink or Lanview.

description: It’s a great tool for those that don’t wanna run too many simulations
author: Mohammadreza K.
pros: Easy to use and super fast and available form anywhere specially when you work from home
cons: It’s very hard to set up and link to your license

author: Ashish J.
pros: All features in one single server. Server is fast and all jobs are carried out without any issue.
cons: Starting jobs by taking file from older RDP session doesn't start.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Julien C.
pros: Versatility, Flexibility. Modern. Excellent Tech Support.
cons: Limited by the internet speed. Downloading the results is quite painfull.

review_title: Beta product not ready for serious use
author: John S.
pros: It is cloud based which is better than on-premise software
cons: Lots of issues and bugs, product is not ready for serious engineering use

description: Solving FEA/CFD problems that we otherwise could not afford to.
author: Jason H.
pros: Access to full Ansys simulation packages for modest price
cons: Licensing verification is touchy, something often seems to need to be updated or fixed. Good support helps bridge the gap.

review_title: User experience Review on Ansys Cloud
description: Ansys Cloud provide us the flexibility to expand our hardware capability whenever we needed.
author: Wing Yew W.
pros: Seamlessly integration into the workbench, make the adaption to use the cloud with workbench very easy
cons: The difficulty to deploy/implement BYOL feature with existing license pool that we owned. A lot of hassle need to be done and setup before it can be use. I will expect or wish to have a simple GUI to allow user choose to use their own licenses or not, whenever they needed.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Heidi Y.
pros: The interface is very user-friendly and the platform connects with different ANSYS licenses seamlessly.
cons: Hoping the pricing will be more clear since the usage is very customizable
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Useful but need to integrate LS-DYNA solver capabilities to the system
author: Sina K.
pros: Support from the ANSYS team was really helpful.
cons: The developer team should integrate LS-DYNA running capabilities since LS-DYNA was bought out by ANSYS.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial
